one_time_description: Settlement proceeds recognized in prior year (2023: $22,321,059) related to Agricultural Benefits Settlement; 2023 also included $13,948,429 in Agricultural Benefits Trust expenses (per capita distributions of $13,350,000)
economic_notes: Nation is a member of Lesser Slave Lake Indian Regional Council (LSLIRC) which negotiates funding agreements with Government of Canada. Subsidiaries include 327938 Alberta Ltd., Kapawe'no First Nation Oil & Gas Company Limited, Kapawe'no First Nation Childcare Society, Kapawe'no First Nation Wellness Services Society, and Kapawe'no First Nation Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust. Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust holds Canadian mutual fund investments.
notes: Five consolidated subsidiaries including two corporations, two societies, and a trust. Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust invests in Canadian mutual funds measured at fair value (Level 2); unrealized gains of $440,199 recognized in remeasurement gains. Prior year (2023) included $22,321,059 in settlement proceeds and $13,350,000 in per capita distributions from the Agricultural Benefits Trust, making 2023 figures non-comparable. Long-term debt (Peace Hills Trust housing loan) fully repaid during 2024. No schedule of remuneration included in financial statements provided. OSR calculated as total revenue minus LSLIRC ($8,077,318) + ISC Health ($3,041,669) + Province of Alberta ($564,397) + Federal DNR ($0) = $11,683,384 in government transfers; OSR = $16,766,754 - $11,683,384 = $5,083,370. Restricted cash includes externally restricted (Province of Alberta $292,600) and internally restricted special projects ($3,097,428).
